## 1. New LLM Versions / Major AI Lab Tools
- 2026-03-31 | [Build with Veo 3.1 Lite, our most cost-effective video generation model](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/ai/veo-3-1-lite/) | Google introduced Veo 3.1 Lite as a lower-cost video generation model for developers. Why it matters: this is a price/performance move in generative video, aimed at broader production use rather than flagship demos.

## 3. Major Financial AI Company News
- 2026-03-31 | [CoreWeave Closes Landmark $8.5 Billion Financing Facility](https://www.stocktitan.net/news/CRWV/core-weave-closes-landmark-8-5-billion-financing-facility-achieving-jit8iphwap7l.html) | CoreWeave secured an $8.5 billion delayed-draw term loan facility, described as investment-grade-rated and backed by HPC infrastructure plus a customer contract. Why it matters: large-scale AI infrastructure financing remains open, reinforcing continued capital formation around GPU cloud capacity.
- 2026-03-30 | [Rebellions Closes $400 Million Pre-IPO and Launches RebelRack and RebelPOD](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/rebellions-closes-400-million-pre-ipo-and-launches-rebelrack-and-rebelpod-to-accelerate-global-expansion-302728631.html) | South Korea’s Rebellions raised $400 million pre-IPO, bringing total funding to $850 million, and paired the raise with production-scale inference infrastructure products. Why it matters: capital is still flowing to non-US AI silicon players, especially those moving up-stack into deployable systems.
- 2026-03-31 | [Diagens Biotechnology Raises US$101 Million in Hong Kong IPO](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/diagens-biotechnology-raises-us101-million-in-hong-kong-ipo-shares-more-than-double-on-first-day-of-trading-302729852.html) | Medical-imaging AI company Diagens raised about $101 million in Hong Kong and more than doubled on its first trading day. Why it matters: public-market appetite remains available for AI application companies with a focused healthcare thesis.

## 4. Major AI Data Center News
- 2026-03-30 | [Gide Advises Mistral AI on Its First Financing, Amounting to Approximately USD 830 Million, to Operate a Data Center in the Paris Region](https://www.gide.com/en/news-insights/gide-advises-mistral-ai-on-its-first-financing-amounting-to-approximately-usd-830-million-to-operate-a-data-center-in-the-paris-region-in-bruyeres-le-chatel-france/) | Mistral AI secured roughly $830 million in debt financing to operate a Paris-region data center and acquire 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPUs. Why it matters: this is a significant sovereign-compute buildout in Europe tied directly to a leading model developer.
- 2026-03-31 | [Oracle Unveils AI Data Platform for US Federal Government](https://www.stocktitan.net/news/ORCL/oracle-unveils-ai-data-platform-for-us-federal-l47g7kfsbx21.html) | Oracle launched a federal AI data platform combining OCI, Oracle Autonomous AI Database, Oracle Analytics, and OCI Enterprise AI for secure government environments. Why it matters: AI infrastructure is moving deeper into regulated federal deployments, not just commercial clouds.

## 5. Regulation & Policy
- 2026-03-30 | [California Executive Order N-5-26 on Trusted AI Procurement](https://www.gov.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/3.30-FINAL-Trusted-AI-Procurement-EO-N-5-26.pdf) | California ordered new AI-related certifications for state contractors within 120 days, including attestations on illegal content, harmful bias, civil-rights risk, and misuse prevention; it also called for watermarking guidance. Why it matters: procurement rules can become de facto compliance requirements for vendors selling AI systems into government.

## 9. Podcast Highlights
- 2026-03-31 | [The State of AI - Q2 2026](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LZeMZgJszKU) | Topic: enterprise agentic systems. The podcast’s core claim is that tool-using agents are moving enterprises from pilots to production. Available now: yes. Use: deploy agents on bounded coding and workflow tasks, keep tool access scoped, and require human review before production actions. Why it matters: the operational center of gravity is shifting from chat interfaces to execution-capable systems.
- 2026-03-31 | [The State of AI - Q2 2026](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LZeMZgJszKU), [Shifting to AI model customization is an architectural imperative](https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/03/31/1134762/shifting-to-ai-model-customization-is-an-architectural-imperative/) | Topic: customization over frontier delta. Available now: yes. Use: benchmark on domain tasks, then adapt models to proprietary data and workflow constraints instead of relying on generic model upgrades. Why it matters: the brief and the linked analysis both point to architecture and domain adaptation as the main remaining source of step-change performance.
- 2026-03-31 | [The State of AI - Q2 2026](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LZeMZgJszKU), [AI benchmarks are broken. Here’s what we need instead.](https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/03/31/1134833/ai-benchmarks-are-broken-heres-what-we-need-instead/) | Topic: benchmark drift. Available now: yes. Use: evaluate systems on task completion, reliability, security, and operator workload on real workflows, not headline benchmark scores alone. Why it matters: benchmark-led procurement is becoming a weaker proxy for deployment value.
